name: Recover – Business Continuity & Resilience
description: Capabilities to restore services, maintain business continuity and improve resilience after a cybersecurity incident or disruption.

maturity_scale.0: Not implemented – no recovery or continuity planning exists.
maturity_scale.1: Ad hoc – recovery handled informally after incidents.
maturity_scale.2: Partially defined – some recovery measures exist but lack structure or testing.
maturity_scale.3: Defined and implemented – documented business continuity and recovery plans in place.
maturity_scale.4: Measured and monitored – plans tested and improved based on lessons learned.
maturity_scale.5: Optimized – resilience embedded into culture with proactive continuity management and automation.

questions.text: Is there a formally documented and approved Business Continuity Plan (BCP) that includes cybersecurity incidents?
questions.description: Assesses whether continuity planning covers IT, data and cybersecurity disruptions and defines recovery objectives.

questions.text: Are Recovery Time Objectives (RTOs) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPOs) defined for critical systems and processes?
questions.description: Evaluates whether the organization has established measurable recovery targets to prioritize restoration activities.

questions.text: Are backup and recovery procedures tested periodically to verify effectiveness?
questions.description: Checks whether restoration from backups is tested to ensure systems and data can be recovered within defined RTO/RPO.

questions.text: Are business continuity and disaster recovery responsibilities assigned and communicated?
questions.description: Assesses whether key roles and responsibilities for recovery and continuity are defined and understood.

questions.text: Are post-incident recovery activities reviewed to identify improvements in resilience and readiness?
questions.description: Evaluates whether lessons learned after recovery efforts are systematically analyzed and acted upon.

questions.text: Is resilience integrated into strategic planning, supplier management and infrastructure design?
questions.description: Checks whether resilience and continuity principles are embedded into ongoing business and IT planning, not treated as a separate process.

recommendations.title: Develop and approve a Business Continuity Plan including cybersecurity scenarios
recommendations.description: No formal BCP exists or it does not address cyber-related disruptions, limiting coordinated recovery.
recommendations.improvement_tips: Create a Business Continuity Plan covering critical processes, systems, contacts and recovery steps for cyber incidents. Obtain executive approval and integrate with the incident response plan. Review annually.
recommendations.nist_ref: NIST CSF 2.0 – RC.RP (Incident Recovery Plan Execution)

recommendations.title: Define measurable recovery objectives for critical systems
recommendations.description: Recovery priorities are undefined, causing delays and misaligned restoration during outages.
recommendations.improvement_tips: Identify critical systems and data. Define RTO (time to restore) and RPO (acceptable data loss) for each. Communicate these targets to IT and business units and align backup strategies accordingly.
recommendations.nist_ref: NIST CSF 2.0 – RC.RP (Incident Recovery Plan Execution)

recommendations.title: Test and validate backup and recovery procedures regularly
recommendations.description: Backups exist but recovery processes are untested or unreliable, risking extended downtime.
recommendations.improvement_tips: Conduct periodic restoration tests for key systems. Verify data integrity and alignment with RTO/RPO. Document test results and corrective actions. Use immutable or off-site storage for resilience.
recommendations.nist_ref: NIST CSF 2.0 – RC.RP (Incident Recovery Plan Execution)

recommendations.title: Assign and communicate continuity and recovery responsibilities
recommendations.description: Unclear ownership slows decision-making during recovery efforts.
recommendations.improvement_tips: Define continuity and recovery roles (e.g., Business Continuity Manager, IT Recovery Lead). Document responsibilities and escalation hierarchy in the BCP and ensure staff awareness through training and exercises.

recommendations.title: Review post-incident recovery and apply improvements
recommendations.description: Recovery activities end without lessons-learned analysis, reducing long-term resilience.
recommendations.improvement_tips: After each major incident or test, conduct structured debriefs. Identify what worked, what failed and update BCP, infrastructure and training accordingly. Track implementation of improvements.

recommendations.title: Integrate resilience into strategic planning and supply chain
recommendations.description: Resilience considerations are reactive and not embedded in long-term planning or supplier management.
recommendations.improvement_tips: Include resilience and continuity criteria in project planning, supplier onboarding and infrastructure design. Evaluate single-points-of-failure and implement redundancy where feasible.
recommendations.nist_ref: NIST CSF 2.0 – RC.RP (Incident Recovery Plan Execution), GV.SC (Supply Chain Risk Management)
